questions.question: What is food security?
questions.answer: Food security means availability, accessibility, and affordability of food to all people at all times.

questions.question: Name the three dimensions of food security.
questions.answer: The three dimensions are Availability, Accessibility, and Affordability.

questions.question: What is 'availability of food'?
questions.answer: It means food production within the country, food imports, and the stock stored in government granaries.

questions.question: What is 'accessibility of food'?
questions.answer: Accessibility means food is within reach of every person without any barrier.

questions.question: What is 'affordability of food'?
questions.answer: Affordability implies that an individual has enough money to buy sufficient, safe, and nutritious food.

questions.question: Which section of society is most food insecure in India?
questions.answer: The poorest sections of society, including landless laborers, traditional artisans, and casual workers, are most food insecure.

questions.question: Define 'Famine'.
questions.answer: Famine is characterized by wide-spread deaths due to starvation and epidemics caused by forced use of contaminated water or decaying food.

questions.question: Which was the most devastating famine that occurred in India?
questions.answer: The Famine of Bengal in 1943 was the most devastating, killing thirty lakh people.

questions.question: What is chronic hunger?
questions.answer: Chronic hunger is a consequence of diets persistently inadequate in terms of quantity and/or quality.

questions.question: What is seasonal hunger?
questions.answer: Seasonal hunger is related to cycles of food growing and harvesting, common in rural areas.

questions.question: What was the Green Revolution in India?
questions.answer: It was a period of high agricultural growth in the late 1960s, primarily in wheat and rice, making India self-sufficient in foodgrains.

questions.question: Name the two components of the food security system in India.
questions.answer: 1. Buffer Stock 2. Public Distribution System (PDS).

questions.question: What is 'Buffer Stock'?
questions.answer: Buffer Stock is the stock of foodgrains (wheat and rice) procured by the government through the Food Corporation of India (FCI).

questions.question: What is the Food Corporation of India (FCI)?
questions.answer: FCI is the government agency responsible for procuring and storing foodgrains in India.

questions.question: What is Minimum Support Price (MSP)?
questions.answer: MSP is the pre-announced price paid by the government to farmers for their crops to encourage production.

questions.question: What is the 'Issue Price'?
questions.answer: It is the price at which the government sells foodgrains from its buffer stock to the poorer sections of society.

questions.question: What is the Public Distribution System (PDS)?
questions.answer: PDS is the system of distributing foodgrains from FCI godowns to the poor through ration shops.

questions.question: What are 'Fair Price Shops'?
questions.answer: Ration shops are also known as Fair Price Shops; they keep stocks of foodgrains, sugar, and kerosene oil.

questions.question: What is the full form of AAY?
questions.answer: Antyodaya Anna Yojana.

questions.question: Who are targeted by the Antyodaya Anna Yojana?
questions.answer: The 'poorest of the poor' are targeted by this scheme.

questions.question: What is the target group for the Annapurna Scheme (APS)?
questions.answer: Indigent senior citizens who are not covered by the National Old Age Pension Scheme.

questions.question: What is a 'Subsidy'?
questions.answer: A subsidy is a payment that the government makes to a producer to supplement the market price of a commodity.

questions.question: Name a cooperative that provides milk and vegetables at controlled prices in Delhi.
questions.answer: Mother Dairy is a significant cooperative in Delhi.

questions.question: Which cooperative in Gujarat has brought about the White Revolution?
questions.answer: Amul is the cooperative responsible for the White Revolution.

questions.question: What is the main role of the Academy of Development Science (ADS) in Maharashtra?
questions.answer: ADS has facilitated a network of NGOs for setting up grain banks in different regions.
